task_completion.description: The YDBGui web interface is open and accessible in the browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser (e.g., Firefox) is open and displaying the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: The agent has navigated to the ^SRF global (Surgery file) within YDBGui.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface shows '^SRF' selected or displayed in the global navigation tree or viewing area.

task_completion.description: Surgical case entries within the ^SRF global are expanded to show sub-nodes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tree view or data view in YDBGui shows expanded nodes under ^SRF, revealing internal entry numbers (IENs) and their child nodes.

task_completion.description: Operative details such as patient/date information, procedure names, or surgeon pointers are visible for the expanded cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Data values corresponding to patient details, dates, procedures, or surgeons are visible in the YDBGui data pane for the expanded ^SRF entries.

task_completion.description: At least 3 different surgical case entries are expanded and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 3 distinct top-level nodes (IENs) under ^SRF are expanded simultaneously or across the screenshots, showing their respective operative details.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the YDBGui web interface rather than querying the database directly via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the YDBGui web interface in a browser, not a terminal window running mumps/yottadb commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually navigated the interface rather than just opening a text file or writing a script to print the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser shows the actual YDBGui application with interactive elements (tree nodes, data tables) rather than a static text editor or terminal output.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type '^SRF' into a text document or search bar without actually loading the database global.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ^SRF data is displayed within the structured YDBGui data viewer, showing actual database contents (nodes and values).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target interface is the YDBGui web interface. The correct VistA database global for surgical case records is ^SRF. The agent must expand at least 3 different surgical case entries (IENs) under ^SRF to view operative details.
